title: Cycle Detection in a Graph with Edge Weights

statement.markdown: Given a directed graph with edge weights and a source node, detect whether there is a cycle in the graph. The graph is represented as an adjacency list with nodes as keys and lists of tuples (neighbor, weight) as values. Implement a function to return true if a cycle exists and false otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: Adjacency list with nodes as keys and lists of tuples (neighbor, weight) as values
statement.outputFormat: True if a cycle exists, False otherwise
statement.notes: Use DFS to traverse the graph. If a back edge is encountered, a cycle is detected.

hints.text: Use DFS to traverse the graph and detect cycles.
A back edge in DFS indicates a cycle.

editorial.title: Cycle Detection in a Graph with Edge Weights
editorial.intuition: Use DFS to traverse the graph.
editorial.optimalSolution: Perform DFS on the graph, keeping track of visited nodes and recursion stack. If a back edge is encountered, a cycle is detected.
commonMistakes: Not properly handling back edges in DFS
Not keeping track of recursion stack

functionSignature.cpp: bool is_cyclic(vector<unordered_map<int, pair<int, int>>> &graph, int source);
functionSignature.java: public boolean is_cyclic(Map<Integer, List<Pair<Integer, Integer>>> graph, int source);
functionSignature.python: def is_cyclic(self, graph, source: int) -> bool:
